front: What is lyophilization and why is it used for peptide formulation?
back: Lyophilization (freeze-drying) removes water by sublimation under vacuum. For peptides: 1) Extends shelf life (prevents hydrolysis), 2) Improves stability (reduces aggregation), 3) Enables room temperature storage, 4) Allows precise dosing upon reconstitution. Critical parameters: freezing rate, primary/secondary drying temperatures.
